description_en: A traditional Chinese blue-and-white porcelain vase (Qinghua) with a bulbous belly, tapering shoulder, narrow neck, and flared rim. Features the classic underglaze cobalt-blue decoration style on a white porcelain body.

components.name: foot_ring
components.description: The base ring (圈足) providing stable footing
components.position_note: bottom of the vase, flat on ground

components.name: lower_body
components.description: Lower trunk tapering outward from foot to belly
components.position_note: sitting atop the foot ring, bottom diameter 0.10m, top diameter 0.20m

components.name: belly
components.description: The bulbous midsection (腹), widest part of the vessel
components.position_note: center of the vase, equator aligned with top of lower_body

components.name: shoulder
components.description: Tapering section from belly to neck (肩)
components.position_note: above belly, bottom diameter 0.20m, top diameter 0.06m

components.name: rim
components.description: Flared lip (口沿) at the opening

style_notes: Classic Qinghua (blue-and-white) style: White porcelain body with painted cobalt-blue underglaze decoration. Typical decorative bands: wave or lotus-petal pattern at the base, main motif (dragons, phoenix, or scrolling lotus) on the belly, cloud-collar pattern at the shoulder, and bamboo or ruyi motifs on the neck. Surface is high-gloss glazed ceramic. No carved relief; all decoration is flat painted pigment under the clear glaze. Proportions follow the 'upper smaller than lower' (上小下大) rule for stability.
